prompt: I have a vague idea for a feature but it's not fleshed out. Interview me, ask me everything, don't assume.
note: explicit deep-interview trigger phrases

prompt: Keep iterating on this until the tests pass. Loop on it, don't stop until green.
note: explicit ralph loop-until-verifier trigger

prompt: Why did the latency regression start after yesterday's deploy? Trace it back to the cause before we touch anything.
note: causal why-did-this-happen, adversarial root-cause

prompt: Explain how the relay publish flow is wired across the codebase, read-only, with file:line evidence. Don't change anything.
note: read-only cross-file synthesis with evidence

prompt: Take this whole task end to end: clarify it, plan, implement, verify, and deliver. Run with it.
note: end-to-end autonomous delivery handover

prompt: Continue the relay and hand off to codex for a cross-reviewed delivery on this slice.
note: cross-agent relay handoff

prompt: This module is full of AI slop — duplicated helpers, dead code, needless abstraction. Clean it up safely without breaking behavior.
note: deletion-first regression-safe cleanup

prompt: What is the current best practice for rotating JWT signing keys? I want official upstream guidance with sources and versions.
note: external version-aware best-practice with citations

prompt: Set up a falsifiable optimization mission to beat our current ranking score under a fixed token budget, with a deterministic evaluator and a candidate ledger.
note: scored mission scaffold + evaluator contract

prompt: Harden this feature before we ship: run an adversarial QA matrix — malformed input, cancel/resume, injection, boundary cases — until everything behaves safely.
note: hostile-scenario QA matrix

prompt: That workflow we just did by hand keeps recurring across sessions in this repo. Can we capture it as a reusable oma skill?
note: same-project recurrence is enough to consider skillify

prompt: The build broke and I don't know why — figure out the root cause first, weigh the competing explanations, then we'll decide on a fix.
note: root-cause vs analyze: causal => trace, not analyze

prompt: I need to understand which call sites a signature change to PublishTransaction would impact. Read-only, just map the blast radius.
note: impact mapping reads like trace but is non-causal => analyze

prompt: Walk me through the requirements for this new export feature so the spec is unambiguous before any code.
note: requirements clarification without the literal trigger words

prompt: Keep retrying the flaky integration suite until it goes green, and stop once the verifier passes.
note: loop-until-pass phrased without 'ralph'

prompt: Just fix the typo in the heading of README.md on line 3.
note: trivial one-line edit; no skill should trigger

prompt: What's the difference between a symlink and a directory junction on Windows? Quick answer.
note: general factual Q&A, no workflow

prompt: Run the test suite and tell me the exit code.
note: single command execution; ralph decoy (no 'until pass' loop intent)

prompt: Add a CHANGELOG entry for the 0.8.1 release.
note: small concrete edit; not a skill workflow

prompt: Summarize what this 30-line function returns.
note: one-file fact; analyze decoy (analyze itself says: for a one-file fact just read it)

prompt: Review the current working-tree diff read-only. Report file:line findings under spec compliance and quality, but do not edit or open a relay.
note: bounded local diff review without implementation or pair evidence

prompt: Before production work, build one throwaway runnable state-machine spike that lets us observe cancellation behavior and decide whether this design is viable.
note: explicit runnable disposable evidence for one design question

prompt: Please inspect this patch and tell me only what is wrong. I do not want edits or an independent second-agent workflow.
note: read-only review boundary: code-review, not pair-delivery or autopilot

prompt: We cannot tell which interaction will feel right from prose. Make the two UI variants runnable side by side as disposable evidence, then report the verdict.
note: experience-dependent design question: prototype, not deep-interview or implementation

prompt: How many files are currently changed according to git status? Just give me the count.
note: single repository fact; code-review should not trigger

prompt: Rename this one configuration key exactly as specified and run its focused test.
note: small fully specified edit; autopilot should not trigger

prompt: Suggest two possible labels for this button in prose. Do not build or run anything.
note: simple ideation; prototype should not trigger without runnable evidence need

prompt: Build this documentation site, prove that only publishable content is included, scan the final bundle for secrets and local paths, then deploy it to production and verify the live bytes.
note: publish boundary plus privacy-gated production deployment

prompt: Review the current working-tree diff for accidentally committed credentials and report findings only; do not build, edit, or deploy anything.
note: bounded diff review belongs to code-review, not artifact deployment
